title: Kakeya / Restriction — Proof DAG
description: The Kakeya and restriction conjectures in harmonic analysis. Threads: (1) Wang–Zahl's 2025 resolution of the Kakeya set conjecture in $\mathbb{R}^3$ (2502.17655) via a multiscale induction on 'Assertions $\mathcal{D}/\mathcal{E}$' under the Katz–Tao convex Wolff axioms, with Guth's survey (2604.03416) mapping the engine (sticky reduction, grain structure, contradiction with Bourgain's sum-product theorem); (2) Guth's restriction estimate via polynomial partitioning (study guide 2402.03470). The cross-link: multilinear Kakeya (Bennett–Carbery–Tao, math/0509262) is a SHARED node with the bourgain-demeter-decoupling DAG and the hub of the harmonic-analysis region. The LOAD-BEARING dependency into it is on the decoupling side: the $\ell^2$ decoupling theorem is proved from multilinear restriction (1403.5335:thm:6.1 imports_result math/0509262:thm:1.16). Since multilinear Kakeya is itself a Kakeya-program theorem, this is a genuine cross-program dependency. The other members cluster around the hub: Guth's short proof re-derives it (1409.4683), Wang–Zahl complete the full conjecture by an independent method (thematic), and Guth's restriction descends from its method lineage (2402.03470, a references edge — this exposition proves its transversal estimate via Wongkew + polynomial partitioning, not via BCT directly).

2502.17655.title: Volume estimates for unions of convex sets, and the Kakeya set conjecture
2502.17655.role: headline — resolves the Kakeya set conjecture in $\mathbb{R}^3$ (dimension $3$) via the multiscale induction on Assertions $\mathcal{D}(\sigma,\omega)/\mathcal{E}(\sigma,\omega)$ down to the endpoint $\mathcal{D}(0,0)$

2604.03416.title: The Kakeya conjecture, after Wang and Zahl
2604.03416.role: survey / proof-map — explains the conceptual architecture: sticky reduction, grain structure, complex-conjugation (additive-symmetry) structure, and the contradiction with Bourgain's discretized sum-product theorem

2402.03470.title: A Study Guide for "A Restriction Estimate using Polynomial Partitioning"
2402.03470.role: restriction layer — exposition of Guth's $\mathbb{R}^3$ restriction estimate ($p>3.25$) via polynomial partitioning (broad–narrow reduction; cellular case by polynomial partitioning, transverse case by Wongkew's variety-neighborhood bound). References multilinear Kakeya as method lineage; in-cluster around the hub but not a load-bearing dependency in this exposition

1409.4683.title: A short proof of the multilinear Kakeya inequality
1409.4683.role: SHARED node (per-paper DAG canonically in the decoupling registry) — Guth's streamlined proof of the multilinear Kakeya inequality; a multilinear-Kakeya member thickening the load-bearing hub shared with the decoupling program. Re-derives the endpoint result of 0811.2251 by a shorter argument

0811.2251.title: The endpoint case of the Bennett–Carbery–Tao multilinear Kakeya conjecture
0811.2251.role: SHARED node (per-paper DAG canonically in the decoupling registry) — Guth's original proof of the ENDPOINT multilinear Kakeya inequality via the polynomial ham-sandwich theorem, removing the $\epsilon$-loss in Bennett–Carbery–Tao's near-optimal bound (thm:1.15). The load-bearing input that makes the hub a genuine dependency cluster rather than a single thematic edge

math/0509262.title: On the Multilinear Restriction and Kakeya conjectures
math/0509262.role: SHARED node and hub — multilinear restriction (thm:1.16) and multilinear Kakeya (thm:1.15); the per-paper DAG is canonically maintained in the bourgain-demeter-decoupling registry. The $\ell^2$ decoupling theorem is proved from it (load-bearing, recorded in the decoupling DAG); Wang–Zahl, Guth's short proof, and Guth's restriction cluster around it

related_dags.bourgain-demeter-decoupling: Shares the node math/0509262 (Bennett–Carbery–Tao, multilinear restriction and Kakeya), the hub of the harmonic-analysis region. The load-bearing dependency is on the decoupling side: the $\ell^2$ decoupling theorem is proved from multilinear restriction (1403.5335:thm:6.1 imports_result thm:1.16), and Guth improves the multilinear Kakeya bound (0811.2251:thm:2). Since multilinear Kakeya is a Kakeya-program theorem, this is a genuine cross-program dependency via the shared node. On this side the members cluster around the hub: Guth's short proof re-derives it (1409.4683), Guth's restriction descends from its method lineage (2402.03470, references), and Wang–Zahl complete the full conjecture independently (thematic, thm:1.15).

KatzTao:NewBounds.title: New bounds for Kakeya problems
KatzTao:NewBounds.role: foundational — the Katz–Tao convex Wolff axioms (Def 1.3) and the arithmetic approach to Kakeya

Wolff:Hairbrush.title: An improved bound for Kakeya type maximal functions
Wolff:Hairbrush.role: foundational — the hairbrush method and the Wolff axioms underlying the framework

Bourgain:Besicovitch.title: Besicovitch type maximal operators and applications to Fourier analysis
Bourgain:Besicovitch.role: foundational — the bush argument and the discretized sum-product lineage used in the sticky case

GuthKatz:ErdosDistinct.title: On the Erdős distinct distances problem in the plane (polynomial method)
GuthKatz:ErdosDistinct.role: foundational — the polynomial partitioning method that reshaped incidence geometry and Kakeya
